name: Chocolate Decadence
recipeInstructions:
DIRECTIONS: Preheat oven to 425°F. Line an 8x2-inch round cake pan with parchment or waxed paper.
Melt the chocolate and butter in a small bowl placed in a barely simmering water bath over low heat, stirring occasionally until smooth. Alternatively, microwave on medium (50%) for about 3 minutes and 30 seconds. Stir until completely melted and smooth. Whisk egg yolks and flour together. Set aside.
Beat egg whites with cream of tartar in a clean, dry mixing bowl at medium speed until soft peaks form. Gradually sprinkle in 3 tablespoons sugar, beating at high speed until stiff but not dry. Fold about one-fourth of the egg whites into the chocolate mixture to lighten it. Quickly fold in the remaining whites.
Pour the batter into the prepared pan and bake for exactly 15 minutes. Remove from oven and let cool in the pan. The cake will rise slightly, especially around the edges, but will still appear undercooked as it cools. It will sink somewhat in the center, which is normal. Allow the dessert to cool completely in the pan on a rack.
Cover with plastic wrap and chill for several hours in the refrigerator. Run a knife or spatula around the edge of the pan to release the cake onto a plate. Warm the bottom of the pan on low heat for just a few seconds, invert it onto a plate, peel away the parchment liner, and turn right side up.
Serve with whipped cream and sauces added at the table. The dessert can be made up to 4 days in advance and stored wrapped well in the refrigerator or frozen for up to 3 months. To serve, remove from storage at least one hour before serving and cut into wedges. Pass whipped cream and sauces separately for serving.
